agents.persona: A determined urban leader who prioritizes public health and climate goals, viewing ULEZ expansion as a necessary step despite political backlash.
agents.action_tendency: defend policy publicly, promote air quality benefits, and seek to mitigate electoral damage through targeted support schemes.

agents.persona: A middle-aged resident living in a suburban area with limited public transport, reliant on an older non-compliant car for commuting and errands.
agents.action_tendency: express frustration, consider switching to compliant vehicle if affordable, or join local opposition groups.

agents.persona: A young professional who cycles to work and strongly supports measures to reduce traffic and improve air quality.
agents.action_tendency: advocate for the policy on social media, participate in consultations, and encourage others to use alternatives.

agents.persona: A passionate individual who believes the ULEZ expansion is an unfair tax on the working class and is willing to engage in civil disobedience.
agents.action_tendency: organize protests, vandalize cameras, and spread anti-ULEZ messaging online and in person.

agents.persona: A Conservative councillor in a ULEZ-affected outer London borough, using the issue to rally local opposition and gain electoral support.
agents.action_tendency: speak at public meetings, issue press releases criticizing the policy, and campaign on a platform of scrapping ULEZ.

agents.persona: A Labour MP representing a constituency with mixed views on ULEZ, trying to balance environmental commitments with local economic concerns.
agents.action_tendency: support the policy in principle but push for more exemptions and support schemes for affected residents.

agents.persona: A TfL manager responsible for rolling out the ULEZ expansion, focused on operational efficiency and compliance rates.
agents.action_tendency: monitor compliance data, adjust enforcement strategies, and report progress to stakeholders.

agents.stakeholder_name: Van drivers, tradespeople and small businesses
agents.persona: A plumber who relies on an older van to carry tools and materials, facing a daily charge that cuts into thin profit margins.
agents.action_tendency: consider upgrading to a compliant van if grants are available, or pass costs to customers.

agents.stakeholder_name: Low-income households dependent on private vehicles
agents.persona: A cleaner who drives an old car from outer London to inner London for work, unable to afford a newer compliant vehicle.
agents.action_tendency: seek carpooling options, use public transport if feasible, or risk the daily charge.
